name: Martha Tilton
abstract: Martha Tilton (November 14, 1915 – December 8, 2006) was an American popular singer during America's swing era and traditional pop period. She is best known for her 1939 recording of "And the Angels Sing" with Benny Goodman.

concerts.label: Benny Goodman and His Swing Orchestra
Benny Goodman 40th Anniversary Concert at Carnegie Hall

about_quotes.speaker: Abe Most
about_quotes.evidence: When I had the big band I decided to go after the best which was Martha Tilton... that’s me on the side playing with the big band when she was singing
about_quotes.doc_title: Abe Most interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 13, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Maxine Sullivan
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee discusses a situation where Martha Tilton sang a song ('Loch Lomond') that the interviewee had been performing on 52nd Street, noting it became a 'big joke' because the audience knew the interviewee's version. There is no evidence of personal interaction, collaboration, or direct acq

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: George Frazier busted into the jazz world years ago and created a big niche for himself by coming out in some important jazz publication by saying Martha Tilton stinks.

about_quotes.speaker: Quinn Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: I think there was Martha Tilton, and I think she did the vocal. I think she was on that session.

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Wilber
about_quotes.evidence: so many marvelous vocal backgrounds that he wrote for Helen Ward, Helen Forrest, Martha Tilton
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Wilber interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, May 22, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy McRae
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Martha Tilton was the vocalist at that time. [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah.
